claim: Roughly one in two thousand UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.

result.claim: Roughly one in two thousand UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.
result.reasoning: While the search results confirm that there is a prevalence of asymptomatic variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (vCJD) prion infection in the UK population's lymphoid tissues, and that studies have been undertaken to estimate this prevalence, none of the retrieved evidence explicitly states the prevalence rate as "roughly one in two thousand" or "1:2000". Papers discuss the accumulation of PrP in anonymized human lymphoid tissue samples and the existence of asymptomatic carriers (16362983), and mention "recent human prevalence studies" (24445428). The Chief Medical Officer also announced an undertaking to estimate the prevalence of asymptomatic infection with vCJD prions (18849289). However, the specific numerical value in the claim is not directly supported or refuted by the available evidence.

evidence.text: An asymptomatic red cell transfusion recipient has been identified with prion protein deposition in a lymph node.
evidence.source: Prion infectivity in the spleen of a PRNP heterozygous individual with subclinical variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease

evidence.text: CJD transmission in western societies has occurred by accidental inoculation with human prions.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Variant CJD is caused by infection with BSE-like prions.
evidence.source: Molecular neurology of prion disease

evidence.text: In some rodent scrapie models, peripheral exposure is followed by prion replication to high levels in lymphoreticular tissues.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Kuru is a human prion disease

evidence.text: Iatrogenic CJD associated with peripheral inoculation with human prions typically presents with ataxia.

evidence.text: Transfer of prion from the periphery to the brain in susceptible mice appeared to depend on the presence of circulating B lymphocytes.
evidence.source: Leucocyte Depletion Of The Blood Supply – How Will Patients Benefit?

evidence.text: Kuru is the principal human epidemic prion disease.

evidence.text: Kuru is the only known major epidemic of human prion disease.
